reviews.review_text:
I’ve loved this game, it’s extremely fun and I’ve put many hours into the game.

Something in the game DOES bother me though.

The swords in this game are the bread and butter. They’re flashy, feel fast, and have most of the gore mechanics.

But blunt weapons on the other hand, they feel powerful, heavy, but despite all of that, they don’t feel destructive. They don’t do anything that the swords do.


What I would honestly love to see for blunt weapons is like being able to break bones or even being able to completely crush limbs into giblets.

you kinda run out of vanilla content fast but its really amazing. It gets even better with mods. The modding scene is bipolar, you'll get an entire week of hyper accurate technologically impressive mods adapting entire abilities from animes and then the next week there will be no mods besides tung tung tung sahurs bat.
This game has the best weapons and powers of any VR game so far. You should 100% should play.

This game made me realize that not only do I enjoy ripping peoples legs off and watching them scream and cry for mercy while they slowly bleed out, I also realized that I'm super ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ gay and submissive.

Remarkable. Simply remarkable.

Knights, bandits, samurai and pirates, mages and golems. What doesn't this game have? The weapon variety, the graphics, the modding capabilities. This has to be one of my favorite games of all time EVER, and I will stick by that. 

10/10. Keep it up guys, and show those dang mercenaries from Eraden what real power is!
its fun, fairly realistic and not at the same time. you can take out your anger in it and that is perfect

Being an avid VR player who has dabbled in pretty much every combat system virtual reality has to offer for about 6 years now, I can confidently say that this game has the best combat system I've seen by far. While there is somewhat of a learning curve, especially if you come from an arcade-style VR game background or no VR background at all, mastering the mechanics and relishing in buttery-smooth gameplay is highly rewarding. Since the devs added in-game mod support as well, you can always find a new way to challenge yourself or simply unleash devastating force on your enemies.

Seriously the best VR game that exists right now, I havent played any other vr game that had such perfect physics you can really sense the weight of what your picking up or swinging around. The enemy AI can be a little silly and dumb sometimes but they are also still some of the best ive seen in any vr game.

+plus theres tons of amazing mods like the outer rim if youre a star wars nerd like me :3

One of those games that is a MUST have if you own a VR. This game is super satisfying, the combat feels amazing, the game is fun even without the use of mods. I still think using mods is a requirement for this game, the amount of mods available is unreal, and 80% of them are very high quality (for example The Outer Rim, or Yamato). The mods range from maps, weapons, spells, etc and I still haven't got to the 1% mark of the best mods yet.

Definitely worth the £25 if you have good internet (I bought a WiFI-6 router just for this game) and a computer that can handle the mods.
Actually the best VR game ever made the fighting is fun the exploration is fun all the new updates are so cool and unexpectied i love this game so glad i fixed my VR so i can play again
Works perfectly fine in vanilla altough can get laggy and glitchy with mods the combat is fun and makes you have aura
super fun, especially waves mode, hours of potential playtime.
This game is beautiful, especially for pcvr, the mods make it all the more better, and they are very easy to install.
i recommend play this game it's fun with mods and just for gameplay but request strong laptop/pc

The modding community is great and so are the devs, extremely fun but after playing for awhile it's not much of a sword fighting game as you know most of their moves by now, but the magic is intuitive and fun even though I'm that basic human knight guy in DND, overall the game I'd say is a 9/10

I really like this game because it's just one of the best VR experience there is. The combat is smooth, the gore is smooth, and the mod compatibility is awesome. Only thing I didn't like about the game was that after each update you would have to update the mods, but other than that, It is peak.
Its a fun time waster, it has a story, but im never gonna play it, I dont know if its just me or what but this game has a terrible time running on my pc

Probably one of if not the best vr games out there. The combat, graphicsm updates, boss fights, modding, magic, and much more come together to make an amazing story/sandbox game. The story isn't the best but considering why you would play this game that doesn't matter too much. 8.5/10

The story is a bit lame, but the maps are fun to run through and the fighting is fun. That being said, the physics can be a bit all over the place when it comes to blocking and stabbing. The magic is really fun to use even if gravity magic is a bit overpowered. All in all, solid 9/10 and a must have for anyone with a full VR setup.

P.S. The modding scene is fantastic and there is a lot of fun to be had.

This game is the ultimate power fantasy, to the point that I kind of feel like a psychopath when I play it. It's extremely fun. You get a big amount of abilities that you can use to literally decimate NPCs. The physics are great, the melee combat is great, the magic abilities are great, and more importantly, the modding possibilities are endless. The AI is a bit lacking, to the point where NPCs feel a bit like walking lifeless punch bags and it is more of a sandbox experience, as it lacks a story mode, which can get old a bit too fast. It has a procedural generated dungeon crawler mode, but doesn't come close to an actual campaign/story mode.

Still, what it does, does it pretty well.

i love this game its so good and i love that your still updating it like the new update coming with a new faction playable area and weapons you guys just keep cooking defiantly my favorite vr game one thing you guys should do is make the ai more advance there kinda stupid lol they take a long time to attack and don't have many attack patterns but other than that games a must have for new vr players

The game is great but its a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ buggy mess that no mod or improvement can save. Devs made no fixes since ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ release and you constantly deal with texture tear and memory leaks in the further game. It ruins the experience and I can't believe they never fixed that. Other than that, its fine.
After playing for a while, for some reason after a reinstall no matter what mods i download into the file, the whole game, even without mods will delete my character and save data every time.

probably one of the best fighting games there is. sure, it may take a few months for an update but its so worth it. tons of mods on nexus with an easy way to install them built in if you dont want to search on nexus. the physics arent the best but it can make for some funny moments in sandbox. i usually play with 50 to 100 mods to make it more fun than it already is. i started on nomad on the quest 2, then got a computer pcvr capable. so i have i would say around 70-80 hours if you count nomad.

Almost got true ending then lost my account because the devs hate cloud saves ig
also the update broke the game for me somehow? Whenever I try to boot it on my quest 2 it starts freezing and unfreezing constantly exclusively with this game
Really fun at first but i couldnt get myself to do another playthrough because the maps are so repetitive and buying weapons feel useless

9/10
The visuals are nice
Enemy ai could be better but its good enough
The magic is fun to use and the weapons are really nice
I personally enjoy using axes the most, usually alongside gravity spells

The base game.. Lacks.. its really cool at first but NPC's well act like you'd imagine old internet NPC's act they freeze hesitate to attack and barely present a threat. the story is rather great and interesting as you are invited to explore highly detailed dungeons even if its the same two dozen rooms copy and pasted.
the physics are great tho a bit busted in damage, hell you can make basically anything work.

Now in terms of modding for example i do HEMA so i use adaptive AI which fix's the AI and Makes them a actual threat i love it tho most things can be cherry pciekd with mods

Base game 7.3/10
Mods 10/10
The modding space is so big with near endless possiblitys

If you watched the movie 300 and wish to feel like Leonidas, then this is a perfect game for you, especially for you medieval lovers who wish to become a knight and see the tears of your enemies as they beg for mercy. I've spent 22 hours in this game, and this isn't my first time playing B&S since I had the Nomad version on the standalone headset. This game might have the same levels over and over, but it somehow doesn't get repetitive and don't get me started on the mod the community makes too. 10/10 game and 1000% worth spending.

The game has lots of content for sandbox and a story mode for those who love progression. The physics are one of the best I've seen and it provides lots of freedom. The movement is especially very good.
Love this game, great community and mod support, as well as devs being great to the player base.

Blade and sorcery is the only game i've ever played that turns a random dude flailing in his room into a master swordfighter. The spells, climbing, and boss fights are my favorite parts, although the simplicity of just hacking people with an axe skillessly is still just as fun. If you find combat too challenging, just remember, if you pierce through the enemy ANYWHERE excluding the legs and arms they die almost instantly. The only big downside is that you will get motion sickness no matter what, although i believe it is worth it.

Would've been a thumbs down if I didn't recently set SteamVR to OpenXR runtime (if you experience crashes or major destabilization when you walk into a new room (especially in Dalgarian ruins) please look into that) but this game is genuinely one of a kind. The campaign is a little short and frankly feels kind of cheap but it's still loads upon loads of fun. Mod manager is a godsend, sandbox never gets boring, and it's really fun to learn all of the sorcery skills, search Dalgarian ruins and fight the Hekalis.

It’s an amazing VR game and I absolutely love playing it. However, since the latest major update, a few bugs have been introduced that hurt the experience quite a bit.

For example, enemy limbs sometimes disappear but still behave as if they’re there. During combat, limbs can go flying off in places that weren’t actually hit, while the limb still appears attached to the body. The ragdoll physics also seem to bug out quite often when this happens.

I’ve also noticed an issue with purchased weapons since the update. Sometimes If I accidently drop a weapon I bought, it doesn’t return to my home or inventory, almost as if I never purchased it. Oddly enough, if I buy the same weapon again, the original purchase suddenly reappears in my home inventory.

These bugs happen quite frequently for me now, and it’s honestly started putting me off from playing the game, which is disappointing because I genuinely enjoy it when it works properly.

Overall, it’s still a really fun game with a lot of potential, but the current bugs definitely need some attention to make the experience enjoyable again.

Game is fun and well done. Some complaints I could share is adventure outpost maps are getting repetitive at some point and bugs here and there like weapons getting stuck in walls. Apart from that, gameplay is great and you'll have hours of content.
Super fan and versatile game. Only bad thing is I cant find out how to install mods which is a menu provided by the game but otherwise, it's so fun.
Pretty cool tbh. Played for 20 minutes and just beat women up in an arena. would recommend if the mrs isnt making you dinner.

This is one of my favorite games ever. I highly recommend this game to everyone who has a vr setup. I do think that the base game is good but I love the moding pottential that this game brings to the community.
this game is fun. its sandbox isn't that good in my opinion but its crystal hunt game mode is pretty good. you can do what ever you want too
The only bad thing about this game is the fact that they moved on to another game, other than that ts is peak dawg play it now.

its actually pretty good. you get graphics that are similar to BONELAB, but if you have a bad pc then i recommend changing the graphics to something that your pc can handle. And also you can get mods from nexus or from mod.io.
very nice, fun as heck to mod. Rather addicting story mode
very fun there is lot of content and worth itthe mods are very high quality i love this game

thing... differant? thy should get thee blade & magic vr exlusive game with populer game modes like "Crystal Hunt" and "SandBox". it is only 30[ish] USD at the time of review.
its a good game that all i have to say the mods are fun the game play is fun so yeah if you like a game that can relieve anger :D
I already loved Nomad but when I got this, it was nothing short of magical. The graphics, weapon variety, challenge, and hundreds of fun mods make for endless replayability.

It's worth having a VR Headset for this alone.

Works perfectly on Linux, too!

10/10, excellent stress relief game.
Great game if you wanna feel like a knight/Viking. Also MOD the game its better that way

I love B&S because its a challenge you want to finish and complete. The magic is literally magical and beautiful to look at. Its quality and texture is so realistic, B&S is in my opinion one of the best games ever. There is a sandbox that is fun to mess around in. I have managed to beat the game and collect all power and it was something. The things I want in a game is all in B&S. There is too much to do in the 10/10 VR game and i wan to continue earning all the achievements.

this game is so fun, first i had nomad then got this to mod best idea ever. modding is so easy tho can be buggy, its still amzing. i totly recomed for the endless fun, base game can deff be boring tho.

One of, if not, the best VR game in my opinion. I just started to play it again after a while and my only complaint is that the AI constantly moves left or right depending of where they are at in the center of your view. They strafe constantly which is annoying but the game is still fun

good game very fun but needs more mods to mess around with.
great for pcvr
very very cool, tons of cool upgrades and stuff SO. SO MANY MODS ITS EPIC its great 4 torturing :steamhappy:
its very realtiscy and fun some times no mostof time i like killing ppl In GAME ofc o-o .......

If anyone is coming from nomad and deciding wether to buy it or not, you absolutely should. The graphics and mods in this game are so much better. The combat and stuff feels a lot better too, plus it's easier to mod. I can't go back to Nomad after this. I absolutely recommend this game.
I can punch people with the force of a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ universe collision and also stick a sharp wooden pole up their ass. Top tier game where u can pretend u are torturing the ♥♥♥♥ out of people u hate :).

After recently getting a new pc that can actually run this game half decently, I have been having way too much fun with it. The combat physics feel so satisfying and being able to slow down time to land the perfect parry will never not feel good. It can get a little laggy though if you're in a hectic fight with multiple enemies (probably because I maxed the graphics out like I usually do) but that does not take away from the fun i've been having. One of the best things about this game though is the endless amount of mods that you can download for it. For example the outer rim mod. It literally turns this game into a whole ass starwars game with lightsabers, blasters, maps from the movies, and new enemies which is something that any starwars fan will enjoy. Overall, this game is just amazing and has been the only thing curing my bordedome recently so I would definitely recommend it to anyone who has or is thinking of getting a vr.

i played this game alot and now i dont play it bc my cable broke and my sis is using my vr headset and i cant do nun abt it bc shes gon argue with me abt that and my new vr headset gon be when crhistmas maybe will be idk but i reall love this game and i forgot all the fun when i played that game

game is extremly good, gameplay is good, ennemies can actually dodge and have a bit of braincells 

only thing I can say is just be sure to not have anything around in case you break stuff

might be the best VR game out there and deserves almost all of the praise. but after all those years I expected some issues to be fixed tbh (I encountered most of these in like 2 hours playtime without mods):

- enemies get stuck in objects, like doors frequently
- player gets stuck with legs in holes and you have to wiggle yourself out
- items get stuck on the buy table and you have to wiggle them out
- performance is not that great sometimes, although I have at least recommended hardware
- optimization for Windows Mixed Reality is bad
- everything is still pretty jittery
- enemy AI is bad
- sound design is a mixed bag (mixing seems off and I frequently don't hear enemies stepping behind me)
- stuck in mod menu because "back" button is greyed out 
- visible shiny seams in level design
- armor clipping into armor, like they didn't even try to make most of them compatible (for example shirts and gloves/pauldrons almost never work)
- occasional bad performance 
- having to press spacebar at EVERY games start because of a Unity bug that pauses game (thx FAQ)
- boss fight that can be ruined by jank, only to have you play the WHOLE LEVEL again if u die

I won like 9/10 fights by just running into enemies with my sword pointed at them, they can't do anything about it. then I suddenly had to fight a huge stone guy and when I understood what I had to do I got stuck on invisible rock debris, my weapon bugged out and he killed me. here, please play the whole level again for another shot at this guy, have fun.

support is not that great, too. discussions are locked and they tell you to go to the FAQ site which is really, really long. as you can see there, there are a lot of issues still. I hope one day this will change. especially because fixes include deleting your saves. like, yeah cool I can play the game again, but I have to start over? of course this is related to mods most of the time and of course the dev can't fix all of the problems mods entail, that's fine. but then again every few patches most mods break and there is a huge backlog of mods that aren't updated anymore and are broken forever. I guess the only solution to this would be a sequel which could be coded in a way so that this couldn't happen that often anymore.

all in all this game is still a 9/10 and basically state of the art VR, despite the jank. the ampunt of stuff you can do and the dedication to the rule of cool is awesome.
I gladly paid full price for this, but I'd really love to see more polish in the future instead of more content <3
everytime i play this game i literally feel like i'm the coolest guy on earth who's attacks are flashy and elegant. definitely top 5 vr games for me LOL
holy crud this game is so peak and its even better that it isnt laggy as hell while using air link which means i can play in a big room and not in the small room my pc is in love this game so much

ngl this game is just mid. The combat gets stale pretty quick. One would think the VR experience would make the senseless violence a little less boring over time but it's just so janky and the AI are just too dumb and predictable. It's cool for what it is, but I'd say this is more worth $20 as opposed to the $30 they ask for. Mods at the very least offer more content and a slightly improved game experience (when they aren't causing annoying or game breaking bugs, which is often). 

6/10 - it's at least worth playing when you're a little stressed and need to blow off some steam on braindead virtual humans
The most clunky VR game I've played. Dont let the YouTube videos fool you. Climbing and jumping is like a 2005 game.

The pcvr version is so great for visuals and fun and i do recommend it but you need a beefy computer to handle that, so if youre fine with low pixels go with the vr version. Though if you have a good computer play this one.
By far one of my favorite VR titles. Runs well on a just above a potato, and way better on my main rig.

dude its like good i like the punching and stabs and magic

While the concept of a medieval magic sandbox is a great hook, the actual execution of Blade & Sorcery is honestly dreadful to play. I gave this game an hour and a half of my life hoping the experience would improve, but I walked away completely disappointed; the gameplay feels cheap, and the lack of an interesting story makes it hard to stay engaged. The sandbox mechanics were the only redeeming part of the experience, but even those weren't particularly fun to mess around with after the novelty wore off. In an attempt to save the game, I even looked into modding, but finding quality content was a struggle and many of the mods didn't even look trustworthy enough to install just based on their preview pictures. Ultimately, this felt like a complete waste of money, and unless a multiplayer mode is added to provide some actual substance, I don’t care to keep up with this game any longer.
Overrated honestly, AI is dogwater and only uses ~5 types of weapons, ♥♥♥♥♥foots around you instead of fighting, and there's no point in exploring the maps beyond the main path they generate.

While this game has issues like not saving cheats in sandbox, the combat is so action packed it feels like I'm in a movie. and the designs for the newest factions, ARE SO ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ GOOD like they took the time to add a ponytail and have gold on the outer edge of the armor on the Rakta set instead of making the stereotype Samurai armor.

This part shouldn't really be said but the modding community is the best out of every VR fighting game, you don't have to beat the story mode to use mods like bonelab, and while most of the mods aren't useable because of updates. The ones that are useable are the funniest part of the game, along side the super-slow physics, like larping as Gabriel ultrakill and kill random wave mods or using op weapons on medieval pesants

In conclusion, this game is gonna be alive as long as you can shoot yoda with a gun.
